text:
Tagenpaku (タゲンパク, Tagenpaku) Tweet by Togashi, retrieved 17 Dec 2024 https://x.com/un4v5s8bgsvk9xp/status/1868697918624219482 is a personal soldier for 9 th Prince Halkenburg. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

It is revealed that with the goal of investigating his ability, Halkenburg had the body of the person on his side who had possessed Benjamin's soldier Vict killed, while Vict's body was alive. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 Since Tagenpaku does not appear as one of Halkenburg's pallbearers, Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 it is likely that he is the one to have possessed Vict Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 and have his body killed. Because of how Halkenburg's ability works, if Tagenpaku is indeed the one to have been swapped into Vict, his soul currently coinhabits Vict's body together with Vict's soul, with priority of consciousness going to Vict. 

Appearance

Tagenpaku is a relatively tall man with short, dark-colored hair parted above his left eye and a rectangular face. He has small eyes, a thin nose, and a small mouth with thin lips. Like the rest of Halkenburg's personal guards, Tagenpaku wears a suit while acting in his capacity as a guard.

Personality

Tagenpaku has not been seen speaking or doing anything in the story besides occasionally being seen around Halkenburg, and so not much can be said about his personality.

Plot

Succession Contest arc

On the third day of the voyage, Halkenburg leads his men in a moment of silence for 12 th Prince Momoze. His men, including Tagenpaku, stand in a semicircle surrounding Halkenburg. The unison of Halkenburg's men in action generates a massive amount of aura, strong enough for 1 st Prince Benjamin to feel from four rooms away in Room 1001 and contact a terrified Shikaku, a personal elite soldier of his who had been sent to guard and spy on Halkenburg on the first day, for an immediate report of the situation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

After the moment of silence ends, Halkenburg begins walking toward Room 1009's entrance. Nikoraurusu makes an attempt to stop Halkenburg from making an appeal to his father, King Nasubi, but drops the matter when he sees how upset Halkenburg is. Halkenburg leaves with Tagenpaku, Tagenpaku's appearance in this scene is assumed from the fact that guards that appear in the related scene in Chapter 382 appear to be the same men, only drawn more clearly in. four more of his men, and Shikaku, and instructs the rest of them to continue carrying out their duties. 

Halkenburg makes his way to Nasubi's residence where he asks to see Nasubi. Tagenpaku and two other guards watch over Halkenburg as he speaks with the guards at the VVIP gate, but Halkenburg is ultimately not allowed to enter, and settles for leaving the guards at the VVIP gate a letter with instructions to pass it to Nasubi. Halkenburg continues trying this for the next few days, and on the sixth day, forces the guards at the VVIP gate to let him in at gunpoint. Halkenburg speaks to Nasubi, and after failing to appeal to Nasubi, to assassinate Nasubi, and to commit suicide, returns to his room, awakened and resolved to win, and equipped with a new ability. Halkenburg returns to Room 1009 and powers up his ability with his men; Tagenpaku stands directly on Halkenburg's left. Halkenburg uses his Grimmal: The Dissonance to force the soul of his guard Sumidori into Shikaku's body. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

On the seventh day of the voyage, The exact time this occurred is ambiguous, and is most likely on Day 6 or 7, but the possibility remains that it occurs on Day 8. While originally surrounded by completely unrelated events confirmed to take place on Day 9, information released in Chapter 404 about the workings of consciousness of those affected by Halkenburg's ability makes it illogical for the complete interaction to happen on Day 9. A wide-angle panel of the Black Whale is also noted in the middle of Halkenburg's scene in Chapter 386, which has been very commonly used throughout the arc to denote that an unspecified amount of time has passed. Halkenburg and his men stand around a deeply-sleeping Sumidori, with a machine hooked up to him to monitor his brainwaves. The men wonder what happened to Shikaku's soul, and Halkenburg gives them four possibilities. After some discussion about the difficulties in proving or disproving any of the theories, Halkenburg calls for his men's attention, and explains his understanding of why he was given the ability, and the implications that the reason he was given the ability has on what the ability itself can do. Tagenpaku stands near the middle of the group, between Gihoruto and Guerimori, and the men listen closely to Halkenburg. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the ninth day, Halkenburg's men gather again, to collectively review their investigation and to be briefed on the next steps to take. "Shikaku" walks over to Room 1007, and presses its doorbell. When one of 7 th Prince Luzurus' guards speaks to him over the intercom, "Shikaku" proclaims Benjamin, and promptly shoots himself in the head. While Benjamin and Luzurus' teams scramble to make sense of the situation, Halkenburg has his men surround "Sumidori", prepared to identify who is inside Sumidori's body and progress their investigation. "Sumidori" wakes up, and identifies Halkenburg in front of him. 

On the tenth day of the voyage, Benjamin and Balsamilco Might, his Captain of the Guards, hear Vict, another of their men, struggle in battle again Halkenburg. Following this, Balsamilco has Halkenburg arrested under suspicion of premediated murder, evidenced by Benjamin's soldiers continually experiencing anomalies. IPA members Steiner and Peuckert, temporarily on loan to Kakin's Justice Bureau Investigation Department, turn up in a team of five at Room 1009 to effect the arrest. Halkenburg and his men are unable to do anything, and Halkenburg is led away by the Justice Bureau team. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

On the eleventh day of the voyage, Balsamilco turns up at the Justice Bureau in preparation for his trial against Halkenburg, not expecting any resistance since Halkenburg had been separated from his guards. However, after passing through security, Balsamilco notices Vict and is distracted by his presence, leading to him falling victim to Halkenburg's ability and swapping souls with Halkenburg himself. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 Halkenburg lets his body get infected with TSK-17 found on Balsamilco's body and, very early in the morning on the twelfth day, die. In a monologue, Halkenburg notes that he and his team experimented with Vict in advance before Halkenburg let his own body die; their experiment with Vict involved testing what happened if the body on their side dies first. Through Balsamilco's body, Halkenburg conveys three final wishes to Benjamin: a ship-wide announcement, a send-off by his supporters, and his personal guards to transport his casket. Benjamin grants them, wanting to be able to show that he honored Halkenburg's final wishes. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

At noon on the twelfth day of the voyage, twelve of Halkenburg's men act as pallbearers for Halkenburg's casket; Tagenpaku is noticably absent from this. They begin the funeral procession from the Tier 3 General Hospital, where Halkenburg had been rushed to when he initially collapsed, Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 and are seen making their way up the central staircase connecting Tiers 2 and 3. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Given that Halkenburg sent his men Shedule and Yuhirai to Kurapika's Nen lessons on the second day with instructions to learn all they can about Nen beasts, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 it is likely that none of Halkenburg's men, including Tagenpaku, know anything about Nen beyond what was mentioned by Kurapika over the emergency channel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

It is worth noting that Tagenpaku is seen powering Halkenburg's Grimmal: The Dissonance in his attack on Shikaku, which is a sign that Tagenpaku has been marked by Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast; Kurapika notes that those with the mark of Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast are half-awakened to Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Although Tagenpaku has black hair, he is drawn without his hair shaded in Chapter 376.
